description_long: This object is a rectangular, box-shaped router or modem with a matte, dark gray exterior. The front face features a recessed port area, likely for power or network connections, while the rear face displays two Ethernet ports accompanied by small indicator lights, one green and one yellow. The top and side surfaces are smooth and unadorned, except for a central vented section on both long sides, which provides airflow for internal cooling. The device appears to be constructed primarily from molded plastic, with internal metal components for structural support and electronic function. Its compact size and utilitarian design make it suitable for placement on shelves, desks, or mounted in utility areas within urban environments.
description: A compact, matte gray rectangular router or modem with vented sides, front and rear port panels, and indicator lights.
description_view_0: The front view shows a recessed port area, likely for power or network input, set into a plain, flat face.
description_view_1: The side view highlights a wide vented section for cooling, running horizontally across the center of the face.
description_view_2: The rear view displays two Ethernet ports and two small indicator lights, one green and one yellow, set into a rectangular panel.
description_view_3: The opposite side view mirrors the vented section seen in view 1, with no additional features visible.
attribute_port_arrangement: 2 Ethernet ports in a row on the rear, indicator lights to the left
